qa_pairs.question: What SSH client versions are compatible with the new configuration for Linux operating systems?
qa_pairs.answer: For Linux, compatible SSH clients include OpenSSH_7.4p1 with OpenSSL 1.0.2k-fips (on CentOS 7.5, 7.6) and OpenSSH_6.6.1p1 Ubuntu-2ubuntu2.13 with OpenSSL 1.0.1f (on Ubuntu 14).

qa_pairs.question: Which SSH client versions are compatible with the new configuration for OS X, and how can users check their client version?
qa_pairs.answer: For OS X, compatible SSH clients include OpenSSH 7.4p1 with OpenSSL 1.0.2k (Homebrew) and OpenSSH 7.9p1 with LibreSSL 2.7.3 (OS X 10.14.5). Users can determine their exact SSH client version on OS X using the command `ssh -V`.
